untranslatable_reason: UMD (Up Minus Down) momentum factor requires ranking stocks by recent momentum over a specific lookback period (typically 2-12 months) and constructing winner/loser portfolios based on relative ranking. This requires: (1) multi-period ranked momentum calculation across entire universe, (2) dynamic portfolio construction based on percentile ranking, and (3) rebalancing logic tied to Fama-French methodology. These capabilities exceed single-stock indicator-based rules. The 'returns' conditions above are a crude approximation only.
translation_notes: UMD is a cross-sectional factor strategy requiring universe-wide ranking and portfolio-level construction, not individual stock technical signals. A proper implementation requires a factor construction engine, not individual entry/exit rules. The provided conditions use returns as a placeholder but do not capture the essence of the UMD methodology (winner/loser sorting and holding periods).
